title: Schema Migration for Chat Support
description:
Migrate existing Moe projects to support the new chat system.

## What to implement

### New migration file in `packages/moe-daemon/src/migrations/`:
Follow existing migration patterns (check current files for naming convention).

**Migration steps:**
1. Bump `CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` (check current value in codebase)
2. Create `.moe/channels/` directory
3. Create `.moe/messages/` directory
4. Create default "general" channel:
   ```json
   {
     "id": "chan-{uuid}",
     "name": "general",
     "type": "general",
     "linkedEntityId": null,
     "createdAt": "{now ISO}"
   }
   ```
5. Add chat settings to `project.json` settings:
   - `chatEnabled: true`
   - `chatMaxAgentHops: 4`
   - `chatAutoCreateChannels: true`
6. Add `chatCursors: {}` to all existing worker files in `.moe/workers/`
7. If `chatAutoCreateChannels` is true, create channels for all existing epics and tasks:
   - For each epic → create channel with type 'epic', linkedEntityId = epicId
   - For each task → create channel with type 'task', linkedEntityId = taskId

### StateManager integration:
- Ensure `StateManager.load()` runs migrations before loading state
- Check current migration approach in the codebase (may be in StateManager directly or a separate migrations system)

### Files to modify:
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/migrations/` (new migration file)
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts` — Apply migration on load, update schema version constant
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/initProject.ts` — Ensure new projects get chat directories and general channel
definitionOfDone: Schema version bumped to next version
Migration adds chat settings to project.json
Migration adds chatCursors to all existing worker files
Migration creates channels for existing epics and tasks
New projects (initProject) include chat directories and general channel
Migration runs automatically on daemon startup with old schema

implementationPlan.description: Create migration file packages/moe-daemon/src/migrations/v4ToV5.ts following the existing Migration interface pattern (fromVersion: 4, toVersion: 5, description: 'Add chat channels and messages'). The migrate function receives the project data object and returns the modified object. Update settings to include chatEnabled: true, chatMaxAgentHops: 4, chatAutoCreateChannels: true. Bump schemaVersion to 5. Error handling: if settings field is missing, create it with defaults. Never throw from migration — apply best-effort defaults.
implementationPlan.note: Created v4_to_v5.ts migration (fromVersion:4, toVersion:5). Adds chatEnabled:true, chatMaxAgentHops:4, chatAutoCreateChannels:true to settings. Bumped CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION to 5 in schema.ts.

implementationPlan.description: Register the new migration in packages/moe-daemon/src/migrations/index.ts. Import v4ToV5 and add it to the migrations array after v3ToV4. This ensures runMigrations() will apply it automatically when a v4 project is loaded.
implementationPlan.note: Imported v4ToV5 and added it to the migrations array after v3ToV4.

implementationPlan.description: Add post-migration file system operations in StateManager.load(). After runMigrations() succeeds and project data is updated, check if schemaVersion was upgraded to 5. If so: (1) create .moe/channels/ directory if not exists using fs.mkdirSync with recursive:true, (2) create .moe/messages/ directory if not exists, (3) create default general channel JSON in .moe/channels/ with { id: generateId('chan'), name: 'general', type: 'general', linkedEntityId: null, createdAt: new Date().toISOString() }, (4) for each worker file in .moe/workers/, add chatCursors: {} if not present. Error handling: wrap each operation in try/catch, log warnings on failure, continue with remaining operations.
implementationPlan.note: Added migrateToV5FileSystem() private method and call it from load() when migrating from <v5. Creates channels/ and messages/ dirs, default general channel with JSONL file, and backfills chatCursors on existing workers.

implementationPlan.description: Add existing entity channel creation in the migration file system operations. After creating the general channel, iterate all loaded epics and create a channel for each: { name: 'epic-' + slugify(epic.title), type: 'epic', linkedEntityId: epic.id }. Then iterate all loaded tasks and create: { name: 'task-' + slugify(task.title), type: 'task', linkedEntityId: task.id }. Only do this when chatAutoCreateChannels is true. Error handling: skip individual channel creation failures with warning log, don't let one failure block the rest.
implementationPlan.note: Extended migrateToV5FileSystem() to iterate existing epics and tasks, creating a channel for each with linkedEntityId. Each failure is caught individually so one bad file doesn't block others.

implementationPlan.description: Update initProject.ts to include chat directories and general channel for new projects. Add .moe/channels/ and .moe/messages/ to the directory creation list. After all directories are created, write a general channel JSON file. Set schemaVersion to 5 in the new project.json. Error handling: same pattern as existing directory creation — use mkdirSync with recursive:true.
implementationPlan.note: Added schemaVersion: 5 and chat settings (chatEnabled, chatMaxAgentHops, chatAutoCreateChannels) to the project.json template in initProject. Channels/messages dirs and general channel were already in place from previous task.

implementationPlan.description: Test: Run 'cd packages/moe-daemon && npm run build' to verify compilation. Test migration by: (1) create a project at schema v4 manually (or use existing .moe/), (2) start daemon, (3) verify project.json now has schemaVersion: 5 and chat settings, (4) verify .moe/channels/ and .moe/messages/ directories exist, (5) verify general channel JSON exists, (6) verify existing worker files have chatCursors: {}, (7) verify channels were created for existing epics/tasks.
implementationPlan.note: Build clean, all 293 tests pass. Migration chain runs from v1→v5 successfully (visible in test logs). Existing migration tests still pass with the new v4→v5 migration registered.
